LAMBDATEST:
Overview

Integrating LambdaTest with CloudFuze Manage enables administrators to securely connect testing environments and account-level data using Basic Authentication. This guide explains how to retrieve your LambdaTest credentials required for integration.

Authentication Method

  • Basic Authentication (Username + Password)

LambdaTest uses your account credentials to authenticate API requests.

Prerequisites

  • LambdaTest account with valid credentials
  • Access to LambdaTest Account Settings
  • CloudFuze Manage access

Step 1: Login to LambdaTest

  • Navigate to the LambdaTest Login page.
  • Enter your registered email address and password.
  • Click Login.

Step 2: Obtain Username and Password

  • After logging in, navigate to Account Settings.
  • Locate your Username and Password details.
  • Username: Your LambdaTest account email address.
  • Password: The password associated with your LambdaTest account.

Step 3: Connect LambdaTest to CloudFuze Manage

  • Login to CloudFuze Manage.
  • Navigate to the Integrations Module.
  • Select Add Application.
  • Choose LambdaTest from the integrations list.
  • Enter your Username and Password in the required fields.
  • Click Connect.

Verification

  • Authentication will be validated successfully.
  • LambdaTest account and testing data will begin syncing based on available permissions.
  • Integration health can be monitored within CloudFuze Manage.

Security Note

  • Your LambdaTest password is highly sensitive.
  • Do not share it publicly or store it in unsecured documents.
  • If compromised, immediately reset your password and update it in CloudFuze Manage.
LINKEDIN:
Overview

Integrating LinkedIn with CloudFuze Manage requires creating a LinkedIn Developer application and configuring OAuth 2.0 credentials. This guide explains how to generate your Client ID and Client Secret, configure redirect URLs, and assign required permissions.

Prerequisites

  • Active LinkedIn account
  • Access to LinkedIn Developer Portal
  • Company page (required for certain LinkedIn APIs such as Talent APIs)
  • CloudFuze Manage access

Step 1: Create a LinkedIn Developer Account

  • Go to: https://www.linkedin.com/developers/
  • Log in using your LinkedIn account.
  • Agree to LinkedIn Developer Terms if prompted.

Step 2: Create a New Application

  • Click Create App.
  • Fill in the required app details:
    • App Name (unique name)
    • Company Page (required for Talent APIs)
    • App Logo (minimum 100x100 pixels)
    • App Description
    • Privacy Policy URL (required)
    • Business Email
  • Click Create App.

Step 3: Obtain Client ID and Client Secret

  • Open the created application dashboard.
  • Navigate to the Auth tab.
  • Locate and copy:
    • Client ID (Public Identifier)
    • Client Secret (Private Key – Keep Secure)
  • Store both credentials securely.

Step 4: Configure Redirect URLs

  • In the OAuth 2.0 Settings section, add Authorized Redirect URLs.
  • Example:

https://your-app.com/auth/linkedin/callback

  • Ensure the redirect URL exactly matches the URL used in OAuth requests.
  • Click Save.

Step 5: Select Required Products & Permissions

  • Go to the Products tab in the app dashboard.
  • Add relevant LinkedIn products depending on your use case:
    • Job Posting
    • Apply Connect
    • Recruiter System Connect
  • Each product unlocks specific OAuth scopes required for API access.

Step 6: Verify App (If Required)

  • Certain LinkedIn APIs (especially Talent APIs) require LinkedIn approval.
  • Submit your application for review if required.
  • Wait for LinkedIn confirmation before proceeding with production usage.

Step 7: Use Client Credentials in OAuth Flow

  • Use the Client ID in the authorization URL (response_type=code).
  • Use the Client Secret when exchanging the authorization code for an access token.
  • Use the generated Access Token to make LinkedIn API calls.

Step 8: Connect LinkedIn to CloudFuze Manage

  • Login to CloudFuze Manage.
  • Navigate to the Integrations Module.
  • Select Add Application.
  • Choose LinkedIn from the integrations list.
  • Enter the Client ID and Client Secret.
  • Click Connect.

Verification

  • OAuth authentication will be completed successfully.
  • LinkedIn API access will be validated based on approved scopes.
  • Integration health can be monitored within CloudFuze Manage.

Security Note

  • The Client Secret is highly sensitive and must be protected.
  • Do not expose it in public repositories or unsecured environments.
  • If compromised, regenerate the secret immediately and update the integration.
MEMZO:
Overview

Integrating Mezmo with CloudFuze Manage enables administrators to securely connect logging/observability resources and organization-level configurations using API authentication. This guide explains how to generate a Mezmo Service Key (API Key) and use it to integrate with CloudFuze Manage.

Prerequisites

  • Mezmo account with Admin access

Step 3: Navigate to Organization → API Keys

  • Click on API Keys.

Step 4: Select Service Keys

Step 5: Generate API Key

  • Enter a Name for the key (Example: CloudFuze Integration Key).

Step 6: Save and Secure the API Key

  • Store the API Key securely (password manager / secure vault).

Step 7: Connect Mezmo to CloudFuze Manage

  • Login to CloudFuze Manage.
  • Navigate to the Integrations Module.
  • Select Add Application.
  • Choose Mezmo from the integrations list.

  • Click Connect.

Verification

  • Mezmo organization data will begin syncing based on API permissions.

Security Note

  • Service Keys provide administrative access—treat them like passwords.

SLING:
Overview

Integrating Sling with CloudFuze Manage enables administrators to manage Sling account access and retrieve required authorization details for syncing users and workspace data from a centralized SaaS management platform. This guide explains how to capture the Sling Authorization token from the browser network session and use it to connect Sling with CloudFuze Manage.

Prerequisites

  • Sling account with administrative access
  • Access to Sling web console
  • A modern browser (Chrome/Edge) with Developer Tools
  • CloudFuze Manage access

Step 1: Open Sling Login Page

Go to the Sling login page:

https://login.getsling.com/

Step 2: Open Developer Tools and Enable Network Logging

  • Before logging in, right-click anywhere on the page and click Inspect (or press F12).
  • Go to the Network tab.
  • Enable Preserve log to retain network requests after login.

Step 3: Login and Locate the Session Request

  • Login to Sling using your account credentials.
  • After login, stay in the Network tab.
  • Search for a request named session (or a request containing session).

Step 4: Copy Authorization Token

  • Click on the session request.
  • Open the Headers tab.
  • Locate Authorization under Request Headers.
  • Copy the Authorization value and store it securely.

Step 5: Connect Sling to CloudFuze Manage

  • Login to CloudFuze Manage.
  • Navigate to the Integrations Module.
  • Select Add Application.
  • Choose Sling from the integrations list.
  • Paste the copied Authorization token in the required field.
  • Click Connect.

Verification

Once connected successfully:

  • Sling account data will begin syncing based on the available API access.
  • Admins can monitor data sync status and integration health in CloudFuze Manage.

Security Note

  • The Authorization token provides access to your Sling account—treat it like a password.
  • Do not share it in emails, chats, or public documents.
  • If the token is exposed, log out of Sling and re-login to refresh the session, then update the token in CloudFuze Manage.

BLACKLINE:
Overview

Integrating BlackLine with CloudFuze Manage enables administrators to securely connect financial operations data using OAuth 2.0 authentication. BlackLine API access uses the OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials Grant method. To enable integration, you must create an API application inside BlackLine and obtain the required credentials.

Authentication Method

BlackLine supports API authentication using:

  • OAuth 2.0 – Client Credentials Grant

You will receive:

  • Client ID
  • Client Secret
  • Token URL

These credentials are used to generate an Access Token, which is required for all API requests.

Prerequisites

  • BlackLine account with System Admin privileges
  • API access enabled for your tenant
  • BlackLine API module purchased (if required by your subscription)
  • CloudFuze Manage access

Step 1: Login to BlackLine

  • Navigate to: https://identity.blackline.com (or your company-specific login URL).
  • Login using your administrator credentials.

Step 2: Navigate to API Integrations

  • Go to Admin.
  • Select Integration Settings.
  • Click on API Integrations or Connected Applications (this may vary depending on your tenant configuration).

Step 3: Create New API Integration

  • Click on Create App or Add API Integration.
  • Provide a name (Example: CloudFuze Integration).
  • Select Client Credentials as the grant type.
  • Assign appropriate Security Roles / Scopes based on the required level of API access.
  • Click Save.

Step 4: Collect API Credentials

After saving the integration, BlackLine will generate the following credentials:

  • Client ID
  • Client Secret (copy immediately)
  • Token URL

Store these credentials securely. The Client Secret may not be fully visible again after leaving the page.

Token URL Format

Your tenant-specific OAuth token endpoint generally follows this format:

https://{environment}.api.blackline.com/connect/token

Replace {environment} with your tenant’s specific environment (e.g., production or sandbox).

Step 5: Connect BlackLine to CloudFuze Manage

  • Login to CloudFuze Manage.
  • Navigate to the Integrations Module.
  • Select Add Application.
  • Choose BlackLine from the integrations list.
  • Enter the Client ID, Client Secret, and Token URL.
  • Click Connect.

Verification

Once connected successfully:

  • An OAuth access token will be generated using the Client Credentials flow.
  • BlackLine API data will begin syncing based on assigned roles and permissions.
  • Integration health and authentication status can be monitored in CloudFuze Manage.

Security Note

  • The Client Secret and Access Token are highly sensitive.
  • Do not expose them in public repositories or unsecured documents.
  • If credentials are compromised, revoke the integration immediately and generate new credentials.

  • In the App Registration, go to API Permissions.

  • CloudFuze Manage access

Step 1: Login to Guru

  • Open the Guru login page.
  • Enter your registered email address and password.
  • Click Login.

Step 2: Navigate to Manage Section

  • From the left-side menu bar, click on Manage.

Step 3: Open API Access

  • Click on Apps & Integrations.
  • You will see multiple options.
  • Select API Access.

Step 4: Generate User Token

  • Under the API Access tab, click on Generate User Token.
  • A new API token will be generated.
  • Copy the generated API Key.

Step 5: Save and Secure API Key

  • Store the API Key securely.
  • Do not share it publicly or store it in unsecured environments.

Step 6: Connect Guru to CloudFuze Manage

  • Login to CloudFuze Manage.
  • Navigate to the Integrations Module.
  • Select Add Application.
  • Choose Guru from the integrations list.
  • Enter the generated API Key in the required field.
  • Click Connect.

Verification

  • Guru account data will begin syncing automatically.
  • Administrators can monitor integration health and API activity in CloudFuze Manage.

Security Note

  • The API Key provides access to your Guru account — treat it like a password.
  • If compromised, revoke the token immediately and generate a new one.
HEROKU:
Overview

Integrating Heroku with CloudFuze Manage enables administrators to securely access application data, pipelines, and account-level configurations using OAuth 2.0. This guide explains how to register a Heroku OAuth application and obtain the Client ID and Client Secret required for integration.

Prerequisites

  • Heroku account with appropriate access permissions
  • Access to Heroku Account Settings
  • CloudFuze Manage access

Step 1: Open Account Settings

  • Log in to the Heroku Dashboard.
  • Click your Profile Avatar (top-right corner).
  • Select Account Settings.

Step 2: Register a New OAuth Application

  • Scroll down to the OAuth Applications section.
  • Click Register New OAuth Application.
  • Provide the following details:
    • Application Name
    • Description
    • Website URL
    • Redirect URI (OAuth callback URL provided by CloudFuze Manage)
  • Click Register.

Step 3: Obtain Client ID and Client Secret

  • After registering the OAuth application, Heroku will generate:
    • Client ID
    • Client Secret
  • Copy the Client Secret immediately — it is shown only once.
  • Store both credentials securely.

Step 4: Connect Heroku to CloudFuze Manage

  • Login to CloudFuze Manage.
  • Navigate to the Integrations Module.
  • Select Add Application.
  • Choose Heroku from the integrations list.
  • Enter the Client ID and Client Secret in the required fields.
  • Click Connect.

Verification

  • OAuth authentication will be completed successfully.
  • Heroku account and application data will begin syncing based on granted permissions.
  • Integration health can be monitored within CloudFuze Manage.

Security Note

  • The Client Secret is highly sensitive and must be protected.
  • Do not expose OAuth credentials in public repositories or unsecured environments.
  • If compromised, revoke the OAuth application immediately and generate new credentials.
HUBSPOT:
Overview

Integrating HubSpot with CloudFuze Manage enables administrators to securely connect HubSpot CRM data and user-related information using OAuth authentication. This guide explains how to create a HubSpot Legacy App (OAuth application) and obtain the Client ID and Client Secret required to connect HubSpot with CloudFuze Manage.

Prerequisites

  • HubSpot account with Admin access
  • Access to HubSpot Developer portal
  • CloudFuze Manage access

Step 1: Login to HubSpot Developer Portal

  • Login to your HubSpot account.
  • Open the HubSpot Developer portal: http://developer.hubspot.com/

Step 2: Navigate to Development

  • From the left-side menu, click on Development.

Step 3: Open Legacy Apps

  • Under Development, click on Legacy Apps.

Step 4: Create a Legacy App

  • Under Legacy Apps, click on Create Legacy App.

Step 5: Configure App Details

  • Select the app visibility as Public.
  • Fill in the required application fields.
  • Add the required Redirect URL(s) (OAuth callback URL(s) provided by CloudFuze Manage).

Step 6: Add Required Scope

  • In the Scopes section, add the following scope:

crm.objects.owners.read

  • Click Update to save the scope changes.

Step 7: Create App and Copy Credentials

  • Click Create App.
  • After the app is created, you will see:
    • App ID
    • Client ID
    • Client Secret
  • Copy the Client ID and Client Secret.
  • Store them securely.

Step 8: Connect HubSpot to CloudFuze Manage

  • Login to CloudFuze Manage.
  • Navigate to the Integrations Module.
  • Select Add Application.
  • Choose HubSpot from the integrations list.
  • Enter the Client ID and Client Secret in the required fields.
  • Click Connect.

Verification

  • OAuth authentication will complete successfully.
  • HubSpot CRM data will begin syncing based on the authorized scope.
  • Integration health can be monitored within CloudFuze Manage.

Security Note

  • The Client Secret is sensitive — treat it like a password.
  • Do not expose OAuth credentials in public repositories or unsecured documents.
  • If compromised, regenerate the secret and update the integration in CloudFuze Manage.

Overview

Prerequisites

  • Active Action1 account.
  • Administrator access to the Action1 Developer Portal.

  • Log in using your administrator account credentials.

  • Click Users and API Credentials.

  • Under the New API Credentials section, click to create new credentials.
  • Enter a descriptive Name for the credential.

Step 4: Copy Client ID and Client Secret

  • After creation, Action1 will display:

 The Client Secret may only be displayed once. Store it in a secure password manager or secret vault.

SIGMA_COMPUTING:
Overview

Integrating Sigma Computing with CloudFuze Manage requires generating an OAuth Client ID and Client Secret. These credentials are used to securely authenticate the integration using the OAuth 2.0 protocol.

This guide explains how to generate the required credentials and configure them in CloudFuze Manage.

Prerequisites

  • Active Sigma Computing account.
  • Administrator access in Sigma.
  • Access to CloudFuze Manage with permissions to configure integrations.

Step 1: Login to Sigma

  • Log in to your Sigma Computing account using administrator credential
  • Use your organization’s Sigma login URL:https://app.sigmacomputing.com/login
  • Note: You must enter your organization’s Company Login URL to access your Sigma workspace.

Step 2: Generate OAuth Client ID and Client Secret

  • In the top-right corner, click on the Profile icon.
  • From the dropdown menu, select Administration.
  • In the left-side menu, click on Developer Access.
  • Click Create New.
  • Enter the following details:
    • Name
    • Description
    • Owner (select from the dropdown list)
  • Select the required Scopes by checking the appropriate boxes.
  • Click Create.
  • After creation, Sigma will display:
    • Client ID
    • Client Secret

The Client Secret is displayed only once. Copy both the Client ID and Client Secret immediately and store them securely in a password manager or secret vault.

Step 3: Configure Sigma in CloudFuze Manage

  • Log in to CloudFuze Manage.
  • Navigate to the Integrations module.
  • Click Add Application.
  • Select Sigma Computing from the application list.
  • Enter:
    • Client ID
    • Client Secret
  • Complete the OAuth authorization flow.
  • Click Connect.

Verification

  • OAuth authentication will validate successfully.
  • Sigma data synchronization will begin based on the granted scopes.
  • Monitor integration status and sync health within CloudFuze Manage.

Security Note

  • Treat the Client Secret like a password.
  • Store credentials in a secure vault or secret management system.
  • If compromised, revoke and regenerate the credentials immediately.
  • Grant only the minimum required scopes (Principle of Least Privilege).
